Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet, where we\xe2\x80\x99re big fans of fashion and fluoride. (We\xe2\x80\x99re living in weird times, be good to yourself. R.I.P. Marianne Faithfull .) Today\xe2\x80\x99s email, exclusive to Inner Circle members, is super, super fun. If you haven\xe2\x80\x99t signed up yet, now is the time . You know I\xe2\x80\x99m worth it!

Up front, I\xe2\x80\x99ve got news on changes in the design ranks at Celine (no surprise, given Michael Rider is in that seat now), and the union walkout threat at New York magazine (no, ChatGPT didn\xe2\x80\x99t write Cathy Horyn \xe2\x80\x99s last review on The Cut). Plus, Matchesfashion is back \xe2\x80\xa6 but don\xe2\x80\x99t get too excited. I have details. Finally, I\xe2\x80\x99m getting into all the changes at LVMH and what they portend for its future.

\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8 Programming note : Tomorrow on Fashion People , my guest is Morgan Stewart McGraw \xe2\x80\x94television personality, Instagram star , serial entrepreneur, and founder of Renggli, home of judiciously priced silk cashmere cardigans , adorable denim minis , and not-too-skinny jeans . We discuss her Beverly Hills youth, the troubled state of luxury retail, and why Angeleno fashion is so confusing these days. Enjoy our conversation on Spotify , Apple Podcasts , or whatever niche platform you use.

Mentioned in this issue : LVMH, Tiffany, Anthony Ledru , Louis Vuitton, Pharrell Williams , Stella McCartney, Maria Grazia Chiuri , Dior, Chanel, Schiaparelli, Lanvin, Jonathan Anderson , Fendi, Kim Jones , Phoebe Philo, Kering, Jack McCollough , Lazaro Hernandez , Loewe, Michael Burke , Sidney Toledano , Celine, Guillaume Henry , Alexandre Arnault , Christopher Kilaniotis , Cartier, and much, much more\xe2\x80\xa6

UPGRADE TO THE INNER CIRCLE

We Need to Talk About LVMH

Despite being the largest company in Europe (depending on the oscillations of Ozempic), the chatter this week around LVMH, from the okay earnings to the Tiffany triumph, has been muted of late. It\xe2\x80\x99s going to take a new creative direction\xe2\x80\x94and return to an entrepreneurial attitude\xe2\x80\x94for industry insiders (and investors) to get pumped once again.

Lauren Sherman

This past week was all about LVMH, and yet not at all about LVMH. Yes, there were earnings\xe2\x80\x94better than expected, but far from spectacular, which signaled to the market that luxury\xe2\x80\x99s road to recovery may be longer than expected. (Shares were down 5 percent the day after the report was released.) Then there was the Bloomberg story about the new-guard-old-guard culture clash at Tiffany\xe2\x80\x99s U.S. headquarters, and the brand\xe2\x80\x99s star-studded dinner in Paris celebrating Pharrell Williams \xe2\x80\x99s capsule collection. (I seemed to care more about these two events than anyone else.) And then , of course, came the announcement that the group was selling its stake in Stella McCartney back to the founder, who will exit her agreement from LVMH to run the business, which caused...
